name: Islamic Center of Irvine
mission: Mission statement - To establish a comprehensive center promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence in serving the needs of the community
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: MUHSEN-Certified Organization (MUHSEN (Muslims Understanding & Helping Special Education Needs))

zakatClaimEvidence: ICOI's Community Assistance Program is dedicated to supporting those in need through a variety of essential services, including food distribution, clothing, tuition aid, and financial assistance. Your generosity helps us continue this vital work. If you'd like to make a difference and support our community, please click the button below to donate. Click here to donate Zakat Ul Maal. ... If you would like to contribute funds to help needy families you can do so by either donating your Zakat ul-Maal or your Sadaqah. ... 100% of the Islamic Center Of Irvine's Zakat fund goes towards providing zakat eligible members in the community. ICOI's fund helps needy individuals by providing a rent and utilities assistance program, food and other basic needs, assistance for stranded travelers, as well as covering the costs of mental health services and other healthcare for needy individuals. (Source: https://www.icoi.net/community-assistance/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 26 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 21/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

dimension_explanations.alignment: The center shows medium alignment for Muslim donors by providing essential religious and cultural services [1]. Its zakat-eligibility and community assistance policies further align with traditional Islamic giving pillars [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health with a program expense ratio of 89.9% and 26.3 months of working capital [1][3]. While its capacity-building efforts are significant, the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data results in a moderate impact score [1].

baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc operates as a religious and community center providing spiritual and social services to the Irvine area [1]. In the 2022 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $2,209,994 to support its mission of serving community needs [3]. The center is recognized as zakat-eligible, maintaining a dedicated community assistance program to support those in need [2].

alignment.explanation: The center is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="4">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving categories such as <cite id="13">the poor, needy, and wayfarers</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="14">clarify its specific zakat allocation policy</cite> to provide donors with more detail on how funds are distributed among the asnaf.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the organization due to its <cite id="8">100% independent board</cite> and its <cite id="12">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the center should aim to <cite id="12">achieve a higher Candid seal</cite> by sharing more detailed audited financial data.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="2">high program ratio</cite> and a focus on <cite id="9">capacity building through significant grants</cite> to local partners.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="10">publishing annual beneficiary statistics</cite> to demonstrate the reach of its social services.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support local Islamic community infrastructure and fulfill zakat obligations through an established Southern California institution.
donor_motivations: Value the role of a masjid as a social service hub
Want to ensure their zakat supports local community assistance
Prefer organizations with very low fundraising costs
Seek to support capacity building for local partner organizations
giving_considerations: The organization acts as a pass-through for many funds via grants
Financial reserves are very high, suggesting long-term stability over immediate cash need
Detailed impact reports with beneficiary numbers are not yet standard
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-frequency international emergency relief or those who require rigorous third-party impact evaluations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes but a lack of public beneficiary data and a formal theory of change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The center creates impact by providing a physical space for worship and education while funding social services through local partners.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that community programs are effectively meeting the spiritual and social needs of the Irvine Muslim population.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The center has a $2.2M funding gap and serves a growing community in Orange County.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="3">significant grantmaking capacity</cite> and <cite id="6">exceptionally high working capital reserves</cite>.

strengths.detail: The center directs <cite id="2">89.9% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs and community services.
The organization reports a cost of <cite id="7">$0.00 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, maximizing the value of every donation.
The center is managed by an <cite id="8">independent board of 9 members</cite>, ensuring diverse oversight and accountability.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Center of Irvine demonstrates a unique model of community support through its extensive grantmaking activity. In 2022, it provided <cite id="9">$1,871,500 to the Rahma Center</cite>, showing a deep commitment to local capacity building. This strategy allows ICOI to leverage specialized partners to deliver social services effectively. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="6">26.3 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, which is significantly higher than industry averages. This financial cushion provides a high degree of security for long-term projects and emergency community needs.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc (ICOI) serves as a vital community hub in Southern California, focusing on spiritual growth and social service. In the 2022 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="1">total revenue of $2,209,994</cite> to fund its diverse operations. The center operates with high financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 89.9%</cite>. This ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions directly fund community services rather than administrative overhead. Beyond religious services, ICOI functions as a significant grantmaker, distributing <cite id="3">$2,125,949 in total grants</cite> to support local partners and community needs. The organization is <cite id="4">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, providing a structured pathway for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through a <cite id="5">dedicated community assistance program</cite>. With <cite id="6">26.3 months of working capital</cite> on hand, the center demonstrates exceptional liquidity and long-term financial stability. This robust reserve allows the organization to maintain consistent service delivery even during periods of economic fluctuation.
